---
title: A title
subtitle: A subtitle
author:
  - name: Your Name
    orcid: 0000-0000-0000-0000
    email: alias@email.com
    affiliations: UC Davis
date: last-modified
---

## Example slide

### This is a subtitle

Here we have some text that may run over several lines of the slide frame,
depending on how long it is.

- first item
    - A sub item

Next, we'll briefly review some theme-specific components.

- Note that all of the standard Reveal.js
[features](https://quarto.org/docs/presentations/revealjs/)
can be used with this starter, even if we don't highlight them here.

## A title {.inverse background-color="#022851"}

### A subtitle

Your Name  
UC Davis  
\today

An inverted slide gives you a quick dark-background alternative for section breaks or title-style slides.

## R example

### Show code and output side by side

```{r}
#| echo: true
#| output-location: column
avg_mpg = aggregate(mpg ~ cyl, data = mtcars, FUN = mean)

barplot(
  height = avg_mpg$mpg,
  names.arg = avg_mpg$cyl,
  col = "#022851",
  border = NA,
  ylab = "Average miles per gallon",
  main = "Fuel economy by cylinder count"
)
```

## Math example

### Inline and display equations

Inline math works like $y_i = \alpha + \beta x_i + \varepsilon_i$.

$$
\hat{\beta} = \frac{\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \bar{x})(y_i - \bar{y})}
{\sum_{i=1}^n (x_i - \bar{x})^2}
$$

$$
\Pr(Y_i = 1 \mid X_i) = \frac{1}{1 + e^{-(\alpha + X_i \beta)}}
$$

## Additional theme classes

### Some extra things you can do with this slide starter

Special classes for emphasis

- `.alert` class for default emphasis, e.g. [important note]{.alert}.
- `.fg` class for custom colour, e.g. [important note]{.fg style="--col: #022851"}.
- `.bg` class for custom background, e.g. [important note]{.bg style="--col: #FFBF00"}.
- `.inverse` class for reversed colours, e.g. [important note]{.inverse}.

Cross-references

- `.button` class provides a Beamer-like button, e.g.
[[Summary]{.button}](#sec-summary)

## Want more?

### Clone-and-use slide starter

This repo uses `_brand.yml` for shared UC Davis colors and typography,
plus `custom.scss` and `_quarto.yml` for Reveal.js-specific behavior.

## Summary {#sec-summary}

### A UC Davis slide starter

The Quarto reveal.js UC Davis starter uses Atkinson Hyperlegible Next and UC Davis brand colors
(Aggie Blue `#022851` and Aggie Gold `#FFBF00`) on a white background.
